most_likely_causes: Concept drift due to changed fraud patterns after promotional campaign
Threshold miscalibration causing excess false positives
evidence: Feature distribution for transaction_amount shifted significantly (data drift)
Fraud patterns changed after a promotional campaign (concept drift)
Precision fell from 0.82 to 0.61 while recall remained relatively stable, indicating more false positives (threshold issue)
Introduction of a new payment channel increased transaction volume by 30% (potential source of both drift types)
Model was not retrained after launch (exacerbates drift impact)
Data pipeline logs show no failed jobs (rules out pipeline failure)
less_likely_causes: Labeling errors in recent data (no evidence provided)
Sudden class‑imbalance shift without accompanying feature drift
recommended_diagnostics: Compute Population Stability Index (PSI) and Kolmogorov‑Smirnov test for key numeric features (e.g., transaction_amount) across pre‑deployment vs. current data
Run drift detection algorithms (DDM, ADWIN, EDDM) on model prediction error streams
Segment performance metrics by payment channel to isolate degradation specific to the new channel
Create a recent labeled hold‑out set and generate confusion matrix, precision‑recall curves, and calibration plots
Perform feature importance analysis on recent data to see if the model relies on outdated signals
Evaluate alternative decision thresholds using current validation data to restore precision/recall balance
short_term_actions: Temporarily raise the decision threshold to bring precision back toward target while monitoring recall impact
Deploy a rule‑based flag for transactions from the new payment channel pending deeper analysis
Set up real‑time alerts for PSI or KS statistic exceeding predefined limits
Run a quick back‑test on the most recent week of labeled transactions to verify threshold adjustment
long_term_actions: Implement an automated drift monitoring framework that tracks feature distributions, PSI, and model performance per channel
Schedule periodic (e.g., monthly) model retraining incorporating data from the new payment channel and post‑campaign fraud cases
Integrate a threshold‑optimization step into the model lifecycle to recalibrate based on current class prevalence
Add the new payment channel as an explicit feature and retrain with engineered interactions
Establish a continuous evaluation pipeline that logs calibration curves and triggers retraining when drift metrics cross thresholds
reasoning_summary: The sharp drop in precision alongside stable recall points to more false positives, which aligns with a shift in transaction_amount distribution (data drift) and altered fraud behavior after a promotion (concept drift). The new payment channel and 30% volume increase likely introduced unseen patterns that the static model cannot handle, and the unchanged decision threshold now misclassifies many benign cases. Pipeline logs are clean, so failure is unlikely. Diagnostics focus on quantifying drift, segmenting performance, and recalibrating thresholds, while short‑term actions mitigate immediate risk and long‑term actions institutionalize drift detection and periodic retraining.
